name: Bundles/batch
description: Bundle batch (type=batch) conformance tests: independent per-entry processing with no rollback on partial failure, conditional-create idempotency, and rejection of malformed root submissions (from BundleBatchTests.cs).

test.name: Batch processes every entry independently; a failing entry does not abort the others
test.description: POST a batch Bundle with 4 entries: a Patient create, a GET of a nonexistent Patient (404), another Patient create, and a Patient create-via-PUT. The batch must return 200 with all 4 independent results, and the 404 must not block the remaining entries from succeeding.

operation.description: POST batch Bundle (create, failing GET, create, PUT) to the base URL

assert.description: Batch submission itself must return HTTP 200 (per-entry failures do not fail the outer batch)
Response must be a Bundle
Response Bundle must contain exactly 4 entries (one per submitted request)
Entry 1 (Patient create) must succeed with a 2xx status
Entry 2 (GET of a nonexistent Patient) must independently fail with 404
Entry 3 (Patient create) must still succeed — entry 2's failure must not abort subsequent entries
Entry 4 (Patient create-via-PUT) must still succeed

test.name: Conditional create (If-None-Exist) inside a batch is idempotent across repeated submissions
test.description: Submit the same batch Bundle with a conditional-create Patient entry twice: the first submission must create the resource (201), and resubmitting it must match the existing resource instead of duplicating it (200).

operation.description: POST batch Bundle with conditional-create Patient (1st submission)

assert.description: First submission must return HTTP 200
First submission's entry must report 201 Created

operation.description: POST the identical batch Bundle again (2nd submission)

assert.description: Second submission must also return HTTP 200
Second submission's entry must report 200 (matched existing, not re-created)

test.name: No rollback: resources from before and after the failing entry both persist
test.description: Verify that all Patients from the prior batch persisted despite its internal 404 failure, and that the conditionally-created Patient exists exactly once despite being submitted twice.

assert.description: BATCH-1 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-1 (created before the failing entry) must be persisted
BATCH-2 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-2 (created after the failing entry) must also be persisted — proves no rollback
BATCH-3 (created via PUT with client id) must be readable
Read must return a Patient
BATCH-4 search must return HTTP 200
BATCH-4 must exist exactly once — the conditional create must not have created a duplicate on resubmission

test.name: Posting a non-Bundle resource to the base URL is rejected
test.description: POST a plain Observation (not wrapped in a Bundle) to the base URL; the server should reject it with 400 since the base URL only accepts batch/transaction Bundles. warningOnly since the exact rejection status isn't spec-mandated.

operation.description: POST a plain Observation (not a Bundle) to the base URL

assert.description: Server should reject a non-Bundle root submission with 400 Bad Request

test.name: A Bundle missing the required type element is rejected
test.description: POST a Bundle missing the required 'type' element to the base URL; servers may reject it with 405 (Microsoft FHIR Server) or 400 (structural validation) — both asserted as warningOnly since the exact status isn't spec-mandated.

operation.description: POST a Bundle with no type element to the base URL

assert.description: Alternative: 405 Method Not Allowed (Microsoft FHIR Server behavior)
Alternative: 400 Bad Request (structural validation rejection)

operation.description: Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a lenient server processed the typeless Bundle
Teardown: defensive conditional delete in case a lenient server created the non-Bundle Observation
